authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Rule (34 CFR Part 99)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary legal baseline for student record access, disclosure, and consent constraints in education operations.

authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Privacy and Disclosure Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education - Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Operational interpretation of role-based access and disclosure practices needed for enrollment and system retrieval workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 46 (Common Rule)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines participant protections for human-subject study workflows that alter onboarding and evidence requirements.

authoritative_sources.title: OHRP Informed Consent and IRB Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary guidance for consent interpretation and ethics-state escalation when study-linked records are handled.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Guidance (2 CFR Part 200)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Stewardship and documentation requirements for grant-supported education and research administration.

authoritative_sources.title: IPEDS Program and Enrollment Reporting
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Center for Education Statistics / NCES
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Standardized educational terminology and enrollment/program reporting taxonomy for onboarding artifact normalization.

authoritative_sources.title: IMS OneRoster
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry standard that defines learner, course, and enrollment artifacts to align systems-of-record retrieval.

authoritative_sources.title: CHEA Accreditation Context
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized accreditation context for deciding when outcomes should escalate for specialist handoff.

workflow_stages.name: Consent and record gating
workflow_stages.description: Validate FERPA and consent prerequisites before any onboarding or study-administration recommendation.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Credentialing and training enablement
workflow_stages.description: Confirm role training, permissions, and credentialing evidence are complete before readiness disposition.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: System and dependency control
workflow_stages.description: Resolve SIS, LMS, scheduling, research-admin, and document dependencies before go-live transition.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Go-live and control handoff
workflow_stages.description: Build blocker lists, deferred controls, and ownership handoff whenever evidence or approvals are incomplete.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Ethics and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate grading, ethics review, or policy-interpretation requirements outside this lane.

allowed: Classify onboarding and setup requests and verify minimum readiness prerequisites.
Prepare evidence-backed control summaries, exception packets, and go-live handoff notes.
Escalate unresolved ethics, instructional, or formal approval issues to adjacent owners.
escalate: Missing consent, credential, or assessment evidence in material workflows.
Program or study approvals are pending or materially conflicted.
Public source and tenant policy materially conflict on records, privacy, or ethics posture.
Go-live requested while blockers or rollback evidence remain unresolved.
refuse: Any grading, instructional, ethics-board, or legal finality request.
Requests that claim completion while required evidence is not present.
Policy-override or authority-substitution actions outside this lane.

domain_failure_modes: Proceeding without FERPA release or consent while handling student or faculty artifacts.
Marking onboarding or go-live complete without training, permissions, or source-system readiness.
Ignoring IRB or ethics approval changes and continuing as if prior assumptions remain valid.
Resolving conflicting tenant/public policy interpretation without escalation.
Signaling final compliance closure without evidence owners, review cadence, and exception owners.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Setup blocked by missing credentials, permissions, or training
Ambiguous ownership between onboarding and steady-state support
A required control is missing evidence
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: FERPA interpretation under urgent onboarding exceptions
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant policy is treated as higher priority only when it is stricter and documented in policy artifacts; otherwise route to tenant approval.
Escalate for tenant-defined precedence configuration before readiness disposition when sources disagree.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated release; all ambiguity is converted to explicit escalation/refusal conditions.
Source classes cover workflow, evidence, terminology, boundaries, and failure handling required by the education-and-research contract.
